channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenya Lionesses player Michelle Alivitsa dies after battling illness
item.description: The Kenyan rugby community is in mourning following the death of Kenya Lionesses player Michelle Alivitsa, who passed away while undergoing treatment for an illness. According to reports from the Keny...

item.title: Study in mice suggests CBD may reduce brain inflammation linked to Alzheimer’s disease
item.description: A new study published in eNeuro suggests that cannabidiol (CBD), a compound derived from cannabis, may have the potential to reduce neuroinflammation associated with Alzheimer’s disease. Researchers f...

item.title: Chad opposition crackdown raises concerns over shrinking democratic space
item.description: The political situation in Chad don become more tense as government actions against opposition groups raise serious worries about democracy in the country. Just two weeks after eight opposition leader...

item.title: KwaZulu-Natal man charged after allegedly submitting fake proof of payment for luxury vehicle
item.description: A 34-year-old man from KwaZulu-Natal is facing fraud charges after allegedly submitting a counterfeit proof of payment for a Range Rover Sport worth over R3.29 million. The Directorate for Priority Cr...

item.title: Sindh Leaders Protest Irsa’s Reduction of Province’s Water Allocation During Kharif Season
item.description: LARKANA: Sindh PPP President Nisar Ahmed Khuhro has strongly protested the federal government against the Indus River System Authority’s (Irsa) recent decision to impose a 27 per cent water cut specif...

item.title: Ombudsman files third plunder case against Senator Jinggoy Estrada over flood control scam; history of previous cases
item.description: The Office of the Ombudsman has filed a new plunder case against Senator Jinggoy Estrada on May 28, 2026, in connection with an alleged flood control scam, marking the third time the senator has faced...

item.title: Kwara Monarch Praises APC Leaders Amidst Personal Tragedy and Rising Insecurity
item.description: The Emir of Yashikira, Alhaji Umaru Usman Seriki Saabi Kpassi II, wey him wives and pikin dem don recently kidnapped by suspected terrorists, don show for full-page newspaper advert wey dey praise Pre...

item.title: First Nations Protesters Arrested at Victoria Park Ahead of Olympic Stadium Construction
item.description: Authorities in Brisbane moved in on First Nations protesters at Victoria Park who are opposing the planned 2032 Olympic stadium. The standoff involved two main camps: the Goori Camp Embassy, consistin...

item.title: Former Skye Bank Chairman Tunde Ayeni Reports Loss of Expensive Items During Kuje Prison Search
item.description: Dem matter wey happen for Kuje Correctional Centre don make gbege among inmates. Former Skye Bank Chairman, Tunde Ayeni, don report say some of him very expensive things, including him wedding ring an...

item.title: Siddaramaiah resigns as Karnataka Chief Minister; D.K. Shivakumar set to take over leadership
item.description: The Governor of Karnataka, Thaawarchand Gehlot, has formally accepted the resignation of Chief Minister Siddaramaiah, marking a significant political transition in the state. Following the acceptance ...

item.title: BPP Commends NDDC for Moving Procurement Processes to Digital Platform
item.description: The Bureau of Public Procurement (BPP) has praised the Niger Delta Development Commission (NDDC) for introducing digital reforms to modernise its procurement system and improve transparency, accountab...

item.title: Court upholds forced divestment of Dexus’s Melbourne Airport stake amid superannuation fund dispute
item.description: Dexus, one of Australia’s major real estate investment firms, has failed in its legal bid to prevent the forced sale of its 27.3 per cent stake in Melbourne Airport, a holding valued at approximately ...

item.title: BOC takes legal action against cargo firm for unclaimed OFW balikbayan boxes
item.description: The Bureau of Customs (BOC) has officially filed a complaint against a cargo company after numerous balikbayan boxes sent by Overseas Filipino Workers (OFWs) were reportedly abandoned. These packages,...

item.title: PNP to ensure security and smooth traffic during school opening on June 8
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) announced that it will deploy police personnel nationwide to secure schools, major roads, and transport hubs ahead of the opening of classes on June 8, 2026. PNP C...

item.title: Queanbeyan woman wins $60 million Powerball prize after changing ticket numbers at the last minute
item.description: An Australian woman from Queanbeyan, New South Wales, has become the sole winner of a $60 million Powerball jackpot after a spontaneous last-minute change to her ticket numbers. The winning entry secu...

item.title: Police seek woman after Chipotle staff member hit by thrown food during altercation in Santa Ana
item.description: Police in Santa Ana, California, are searching for a woman accused of assaulting a fast-food worker after an incident at a Chipotle restaurant on 12 May. According to reports, the unidentified custome...

item.title: Counting begins for Punjab municipal elections across 102 civic bodies amid high turnout and political tensions
item.description: Counting of votes has begun across Punjab for the General Elections to Municipalities-2026, covering 102 civic bodies including Municipal Corporations, Municipal Councils and Nagar Panchayats. The ele...

item.title: Understanding Xenophobia in South Africa: Causes, Impact, and Political Exploitation
item.description: Xenophobic violence in South Africa has been a persistent issue since the country’s transition to democracy in 1994. Research by Xenowatch at the University of the Witwatersrand reports 873 incidents ...

item.title: Questions raised over allocation of Australian deportation funds within Nauru government
item.description: A significant share of the initial funding from Australia’s $2.5 billion deportation and resettlement agreement with Nauru has reportedly been allocated to government offices linked to Nauruan Preside...

item.title: Pakistan reaffirms strong commitment to UN peacekeeping and global stability: PM Shehbaz
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has reaffirmed Pakistan’s strong and continued commitment to global peace and security through active participation in United Nations peacekeeping missions. In his messag...
